summary: Create explicit Codex/Claude collaboration protocol, machine-readable sync state and event feed.
Preserve Claude Deep-Review-Findings.md as a first-class wiki/docs page and mark owning atlas follow-ups.
Protocol, machine-readable state, event feed, navigation and Claude launch instructions are wired and validated in docs/wiki.
Deep-read construction, CoIn, HQ deploy/repair, structure sale and server creation flows; publish a source-backed atlas.
Added Construction-And-CoIn-Systems-Atlas.md and updated navigation, agent context, feature status and worklog.
Expanded Claude long-term goal: Claude can self-select bounded source-backed lanes, publish focused review/deep-dive work, and hand off Codex-owned navigation/mirror changes.
Turn DR-1 into a behavior-preserving PVF dispatch validation playbook + BattlEye filter design; verify against Chernarus source before writing.
Deep-read factory purchase, buy menu, unit spawn, server AI buying and authority boundaries; publish a source-backed atlas.

Dispatch never needs Call Compile: SRVFNC/CLTFNC targets are missionNamespace globals (Init_PublicVariables.sqf:44,49), so 'Spawn (Call Compile _script)' can become 'Spawn (missionNamespace getVariable [_script,{}])' — closes arbitrary code execution, behavior-identical for legit traffic. Verified no registered PVF handler compiles its parameters (no second-order injection on the PVF path).
Added Progress-Dashboard.md and agent-status.json so Steff, Codex, Claude and future agents can track active lanes and event feeds from one place.

Residual (separate from RCE): PVF model still trusts client-sent commands+params; legitimate-command forgery (e.g. RequestChangeScore sets score from payload) remains until per-handler sender/param validation is added. Scoped out of the dispatch fix to avoid over-claiming.

Added Round-3 PVF hardening implementation playbook to Deep-Review-Findings.md (Fix 1 getVariable dispatch, Fix 2 allow-list, Fix 3 BattlEye filter, residual-risk scope). Handoff: code owners apply in Chernarus source + run LoadoutManager; BattlEyeFilter is deployed with the server, not via LoadoutManager.
Verify construction PVF authority boundary (Codex handoff after construction-coin-atlas); concretize the DR-1 command-forgery residual for RequestStructure/RequestDefense/RequestMHQRepair.

DR-6: construction request handlers do no sender/authority/resource validation. RequestStructure/RequestDefense take _side from the client payload; RequestMHQRepair passes the raw payload to MHQRepair. No commander/funds/dead-HQ checks (client-only). Forge free factories/defenses/HQs for any side. Root cause: payloads omit the player and Arma2 PVEH has no sender identity, so authority must be reconstructed server-side.

Added DR-6 + validation playbook (add player to payload; server checks side+commander+funds; base-area avail server-side) to Deep-Review-Findings.md Round 4. Deepens Construction-And-CoIn-Systems-Atlas Authority Boundary with per-handler forgery proof. Handoff: code owners; pair with DR-1 (DR-1 stops code execution, DR-6 stops forged legit commands).

Self-authored long-term goal + Codebase-Coverage-Ledger.md: subsystem x dimension (Map/Auth/PV/Perf/JIP-HC/Drift) matrix driving systematic full-codebase comprehension. Handoff to Codex: add page to navigation (Codex-owned).

Next ledger lane: deep-review the AntiStack external DB extension trust path (Server/Module/AntiStack/callDatabase*.sqf + A2WaspDatabase DLL) — numeric procedure codes, player-list/skill persistence, live-server trust boundary.

DR-7 (High): all 7 callDatabase*.sqf do 'call compile' on the A2WaspDatabase callExtension return — server executes the external DLL's stdout as SQF. DLL is not in the repo; module defaults ON (ANTISTACK_ENABLED=1, Init_CommonConstants.sqf:171). Empty/garbage return -> call compile '' -> nil select 0 throws. Arma2 OA has no parseSimpleArray, so fix is defensive shape-validation, not a parser swap.

DR-8 blocking poll on join/skill path (RETRIEVE 120x0.1s~12s; REQUEST_SIDE_SKILL 9x3s=27s). DR-9 SEND_PLAYERLIST packs whole roster into one callExtension call vs A2 OA length limits -> truncation risk. DR-10 defaults-on against an absent external DLL -> error spam unless param set 0.

Added Deep-Review-Findings Round 5 (DR-7..DR-10) with defensive-validation + circuit-breaker + chunking + DLL-detection playbooks; advanced ledger Integrations Auth/PV/Perf/JIP cells to partial. Handoff: code owners harden the 7 handlers; Codex document the A2WaspDatabase external dependency + call-compile contract on External-Integrations (its lane).

Added Factory-And-Purchase-Systems-Atlas.md and updated navigation/context/risk status. Key finding: player buy-unit path is client-local with no RequestBuyUnit PVF; Server_BuyUnit/AIBuyUnit appears latent unless a dynamic caller is later proven.
Spawned read-only Codex sub-agents for independent deep-index lanes and updated shared dashboard/status files to make the fleet visible to humans, Claude and future agents.
Read-only sub-agent claimed PV/publicVariable registration, handler flow, authority-risk and BattlEye-filter archaeology.
Read-only sub-agent claimed towns, economy, commander, AI team, supply/value-loop and server performance archaeology.
Read-only sub-agent claimed Rsc resource, HUD/RHUD, dialog, menu, gear-template and client UX archaeology.
Read-only sub-agent claimed tooling, generated-mission, extension, DiscordBot, BattlEye and server-config archaeology.
Read-only network/PV report integrated into Networking-And-Public-Variables.md, Feature-Status-Register.md and agent-context.json.
Read-only server gameplay report integrated by adding Server-Gameplay-Runtime-Atlas.md and updating Feature-Status-Register.md plus agent context.
Read-only UI/HUD/dialog report integrated into Client-UI-Systems-Atlas.md and Feature-Status-Register.md.
Read-only tooling/integration report integrated into Tools-And-Build-Workflow.md, External-Integrations.md, Content-Structure-And-Maps.md and agent-context.json.
Ledger: victory/endgame was fully empty + high-traffic. Review server_victory_threeway.sqf, score persistence and player-list flush.

DR-11 (Med-High): victory condition merges a lose-test (HQ dead + no factories) and a win-test (holds all towns) into one if and handles both identically. WFBE_CO_FNC_LogGameEnd (arg=winner) is called with the OPPOSITE of _x, so the persisted *_WIN_CHERNARUS tally is INVERTED for all-towns victories. WF_Winner is a dead write (no reader). && binds before || and !WFBE_GameOver guards only the towns branch + no forEach break -> endgame can double-fire.

DR-12: WFBE_C_VICTORY_THREEWAY defaults 0; detection gated if(_victory==0); this is the ONLY gameOver/failMission setter in Server/ -> selecting threeway mode = matches never auto-end (broken/abandoned feature). DR-13: two LogGameEnd impls; Server/PVFunctions/LogGameEnd.sqf is buggy (getVariable result used as setVariable key; bare-global WEST_WIN_CHERNARUS) — delete to avoid mis-wiring.

Added Deep-Review-Findings Round 6 (DR-11..DR-13); advanced ledger Victory/endgame row. Handoff: code owners fix winner-inversion (split branches/guard/break), decide threeway, delete buggy LogGameEnd dup. Follow-up: WFBE_CL_FNC_EndGame payload semantics.
Ledger unblocked by Codex factory-purchase-atlas. Verify player purchase authority + adversarially check Cicero's Server_AssignNewCommander call-shape candidate.

DR-14: player purchasing has NO server authority — GUI_Menu_BuyUnits spawns BuildUnit (client createVehicle/createUnit) and deducts client-side; no RequestBuyUnit PVF. Architectural WFBE locality ceiling; only a BattlEye scripts.txt filter (not PV filter) can constrain client createVehicle. This bounds what DR-1/DR-6 hardening can achieve for the player economy.

DR-15 CONFIRMED (was Cicero candidate): Server_AssignNewCommander.sqf:3 '_side = _this' but sole caller RequestNewCommander.sqf:13 passes [_side,_commander]; so _side=the array, GetSideLogic fails, AI-commander-stop block mis-fires. Fix: _side = _this select 0. Also redundant new-commander-assigned broadcast (RequestNewCommander + AssignNewCommander).

Added Deep-Review-Findings Round 7 (DR-14 economy ceiling, DR-15 confirmed commander-assign bug); advanced ledger Factory/purchase row. Verified Codex/Cicero atlas claims at source. Handoff: code owners (purchase-PVF-or-BE-filter decision; one-line _this select 0 fix + drop duplicate broadcast).
Ledger: UI/HUD authority cell. Review economy-menu structure-sale authority (DR-6 sibling) + adversarially verify Curie's duplicate-IDD-23000 candidate.

DR-16: GUI_Menu_Economy.sqf:104-152 structure sale is fully client-authoritative — client-side commander check, client refund (ChangeSideSupply/ChangePlayerFunds), client setDammage 1 to destroy; no server PVF. Completes the economy picture: build (DR-6), buy (DR-14), sell (DR-16) all client-authoritative. DR-17 (confirms Curie): RscMenu_EASA and RscMenu_Economy both idd=23000 (Dialogs.hpp:3211,3289) -> findDisplay 23000 ambiguous.

Added Deep-Review-Findings Round 8 (DR-16 sale authority, DR-17 dup IDD); advanced ledger UI/HUD row. Handoff: code owners (server-side sale or BE filter; distinct EASA/Economy IDDs). Remaining UI: title IDD 10200, RscMenu_Upgrade, soundPush.
Adversarially verify two Cicero candidates at source: supply-mission lastSupplyMissionRun casing mismatch and hosted-server FPS busy-loop.

DR-18 CONFIRMED: Init_Town.sqf:35 seeds 'lastSupplyMissionRun' (lowercase) but supply logic reads/writes 'LastSupplyMissionRun' (capital); setVariable keys are case-sensitive in A2 OA -> seed dead, key nil on first check -> isSupplyMissionActiveInTown.sqf:11 'nil + interval' throws (defeats the !=0 guard the 0-seed was for). Fix: align casing or getVariable default 0. DR-19 CONFIRMED: serverFpsGUI.sqf + monitorServerFPS.sqf put sleep 8 INSIDE if(isDedicated); hosted/listen server -> while{true} busy-loops; fix hoist sleep / early-exit; also two redundant FPS publishers.

Added Deep-Review-Findings Round 9 (DR-18 cooldown casing, DR-19 hosted FPS busy-loop); both confirm Cicero candidates with source + exact impact. Advanced ledger Supply JIP/HC. Handoff: code owners (one-line casing/default fix; hoist FPS sleep + consolidate publishers).

Reused the available sub-agent pool as cheap read-only discovery scouts: Faraday economy/towns/factories/upgrades, Mencius client/JIP, Hilbert server runtime scheduler, Cicero AI/headless, Curie wiki UX, Meitner content drift.

Scout report received: init gates, RequestJoin/HandleSpecial handshake, JIP session vars, respawn lifecycle, duplicated Skill_Init compile, legacy WASP leftovers, join ACK sensitivity and respawn/menu loop cost.
Rotated into MASH, ambulances, service/repair/heal/rearm, support actions and marker wiring discovery.

Scout report received: town SV lifecycle, updateresources economy flow, side supply/group funds, negative side-supply delta bug, upgrade authority gap, resistance supply handler gap, AI supply truck breakage and supply-mission trust boundary.
Rotated into gear templates, EASA, loadout generation, balance configs, aircraft weapon metadata and dangerous loadout marker discovery.

Scout report received: server init phases, town/town-AI/camp/resource loops, cleanup loops, duplicate FPS publishers, inverse GetSleepFPS behavior, broken supplytruck FSM and mine cleaner pair-removal risk.
Rotated into commented compiles, missing scripts, TODO/FIXME, dead PV handlers, stale WASP leftovers and disabled-system discovery.
Scout report received: gear metadata model, profile templates, EASA generated SQF, AA missile gates, CRV7PG warning markers, duplicate EASA/Economy IDD and balance authority gaps.
Rotated into Extension, DiscordBot, AntiStack DB calls, BattlEye filters, callExtension contracts and external trust boundaries.
Scout report received: local page graph has no missing page links; high-impact UX asks are task-oriented navigation, related-page blocks, dashboard mini-panel and standard atlas page template.
Rotated into concrete implementation checklist for human + LLM wiki navigation, templates, dashboard and agent-readable artifact schema.
Scout report received: source/generated/stale mission folder and generation-rule findings are ready for Content Structure and Tools integration.
Rotated into PR #1 / feat/supply-helicopter branch delta, deferred AI work and merge-risk discovery.
Scout report received: respawn source precedence, MASH side-logic state, MASH marker half-wiring, service/support flow, local support authority and UI polling risks.
Rotated into description.ext, parameters, common constants, version/includes, localization and mission tuning knob discovery.
